content:
You’ve been shipping a decade-old utility library built for browsers that no longer exist. Here’s the modern replacement.

2 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

For more than a decade, lodash was the reflex answer to "which utility library?" It was everywhere, reliable, and quietly shipped in almost every JavaScript project on earth.
But the JavaScript of 2026 is not the JavaScript lodash was designed for. We have native array methods, optional chaining, nullish coalescing, and engines that optimize modern code aggressively. Meanwhile bundle size has become a first-class performance budget: every extra kilobyte is slower LCP, heavier cold starts, worse Core Web Vitals.
es-toolkit was built to close that gap. The headline numbers:

• 2–3× faster than lodash across the official benchmarks

• Up to 97% smaller bundle footprint per function — clean tree-shaking by default

• 100% test coverage, written in TypeScript, MIT licensed

• Already in production at Microsoft, IBM, Yarn, Storybook, Recharts, MUI
Migration is a one-liner
es-toolkit ships a compatibility layer: es-toolkit/compat mirrors the lodash API exactly. Alias it in your bundler or change the import path — existing code keeps working. Then migrate hot paths to the slim main entry to claim the full speed and size wins.
The pragmatic path: default to es-toolkit for new code, keep lodash only for the rare niche function that has no equivalent yet.
